seniority.level: Director / Group PM (IC8 or Director equivalent)
level_signals: 10+ years of relevant Product Management experience
4+ years of experience managing Product Managers
Responsible for long-term vision, strategy, and roadmap for Payments Intelligence products
Build and coach your team on best practices

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Product Management experience: 10+ years of relevant experience
4+ years of experience managing Product Managers
Strong business acumen: You enjoy digging into the details of how a business works, identifying how to make it better, and building product/technical solutions to do so
Customer and Partner facing experience: You're comfortable in front of customers or third-party collaborators, and have experience navigating customer conversations
Operational savvy: You are strong operationally, capable of designing and driving a cross-functional team and process
Analytical horsepower: You dig deep into the data, think from user first principles, and deliver the right results
Crisp communicator: High attention to detail including precise and effective communications, including with external customers/partners
Results-oriented: You enjoy doing whatever it takes to execute on complex projects

scope.surface: Payments Intelligence products — Radar, Authorization Boost, Disputes; ML-driven payment infrastructure and fraud/authorization controls
core_responsibilities: Develop the long-term vision and strategy for our Payments Intelligence products, prioritize accordingly, and create an execution roadmap
Deeply understand our users' needs by spending time with developers, sales, and support, and by observing patterns/trends across their feedback, the market, and our data
Build and launch new capabilities and products that bring significant value to customers
Set the pace, prioritization and execution for the team
Work with a wide range of systems, processes and technologies to own and solve technical and product problems
Collaborate with stakeholders across the organization such as Engineering, Design, Data Science, Marketing, Sales, Account Management, and Operations
Build and coach your team on best practices in defining product requirements and how to deliver against those requirements and the overall roadmap

green_flags: Clear product domain with named products (Radar, Authorization Boost, Disputes)
Explicit minimum requirements section with encouragement to apply if met
High organizational scope — owns vision, strategy, and team coaching
Cross-functional breadth across engineering, data science, design, sales, and partnerships
red_flags: Location and remote/hybrid policy not stated anywhere in the JD
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information provided
Role title says 'AI' but JD body focuses on Payments Intelligence — AI scope is ambiguous
No preferred qualifications listed, making differentiation criteria unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 10+ years PM + 4+ years managing PMs + strategic scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from Stripe's description as a financial infrastructure platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.source (unknown; no comp data in JD)
ambiguities: Role title is 'Product Lead, AI' but the JD body describes the Payments Intelligence team (Radar, Authorization Boost, Disputes) without explicitly referencing AI as a standalone product area — the AI label may refer to ML/AI embedded in payments intelligence products rather than a separate AI product suite.
Location, remote eligibility, and office policy are entirely absent from the JD — work arrangement is unclear.
